title: Apply one pilot Snyk Code ignore policy and verify visibility

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Started: user reported pilot finding not showing as ignored. Plan: check current policies, create one policy targeting finding 983bb4bf-16a9-4225-9394-956896b825e8, then verify with API and Snyk Code test include-ignores output.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Confirmed root cause: prior pilot created rationale text only, not an actual ignore policy; therefore nothing appeared ignored.

Applied one real pilot ignore via Snyk Policies API for finding  (tools/ec2_bench_status.py:10). Created policy id: .

Verification:
- API list now shows 1 policy.
- 
Testing /Users/derek.norrbom/Projects/Virtuus ...

Open Issues

 ✗ [MEDIUM] Command Injection
   Finding ID: 73a117d3-bead-4e45-851d-f4da2d8c80ae
   Path: tools/ec2_bench_status.py, line 87
   Info: Unsanitized input from a command line argument flows into subprocess.check_output, where it is used as a shell command. This may result in a Command Injection vulnerability.

 ✗ [MEDIUM] Command Injection
   Finding ID: 55534b96-f432-459c-ad9a-a77d0dd6ae8a
   Path: tools/run_ec2_from_params.py, line 63
   Info: Unsanitized input from a command line argument flows into subprocess.check_call, where it is used as a shell command. This may result in a Command Injection vulnerability.

 ✗ [MEDIUM] Path Traversal
   Finding ID: 5a20415f-ecb6-4255-9419-998a7294196f
   Path: examples/sakila/build_sakila.py, line 157
   Info: Unsanitized input from a command line argument flows into json.dump, where it is used as a path. This may result in a Path Traversal vulnerability and allow an attacker to write arbitrary files.

Ignored Issues

 ! [MEDIUM] Command Injection [ IGNORED ]
   Finding ID: 983bb4bf-16a9-4225-9394-956896b825e8
   Path: tools/ec2_bench_status.py, line 10
   Info: Unsanitized input from a command line argument flows into subprocess.check_output, where it is used as a shell command. This may result in a Command Injection vulnerability.

   Expiration: never
   Category:   wont-fix
   Ignored on: March 02, 2026
   Ignored by: Derek Norrbom
   Reason:     False positive: subprocess list invocation without shell=True in developer-only CLI context. Pilot ignore applied via API.



💡 Tip

   Ignores are currently managed in the Snyk Web UI.              
   To edit or remove the ignore please go to: https://app.snyk.io/

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Confirmed root cause: prior pilot created rationale text only, not an actual ignore policy; therefore nothing appeared ignored.

Applied one real pilot ignore via Snyk Policies API for finding 983bb4bf-16a9-4225-9394-956896b825e8 (tools/ec2_bench_status.py:10).
Created policy id: 9f5b1d65-bbd2-44a1-a2c6-1c3a13cb9b48.

Verification:
- API list now shows 1 policy.
- snyk code test --include-ignores reports the finding under Ignored Issues.
- Test summary now shows Ignored issues: 1, Open issues: 15.
